questions.question: Consider a dataset containing the prices of houses in a city. Calculate the covariance matrix of the features. What are the implications of this calculation for the discriminant analysis model?
questions.hint: Consider the formula for covariance matrix and its relation to the discriminant analysis model.

questions.question: Suppose we have a dataset containing the prices of houses in a city. We want to perform discriminant analysis to classify houses as expensive or cheap. Which of the following is a key assumption of the Fisher's Linear Discriminant?
questions.hint: Refer to Chapter 5 of the textbook.

questions.question: A dataset contains the prices of houses in a city. The dataset is binary (0/1) where 0 represents a cheap house and 1 represents an expensive house. We want to perform discriminant analysis to predict the price of a house. What is the appropriate type of Generalized Linear Model (GLM) to use in this case?
questions.hint: Consider the nature of the response variable and the type of data.
